user_prompt: This needs to spin up a FreeRTOS task thread on the same executing core as the current executing core
The thread will remember the time retrieved from the server, and then determine how far off the current time is from RTC is from NTP time.
There is a public call for initiating this, another call for checking status (idle, busy, done, error), another call for cancelling, and another call for retreving the error.
There needs to be a public call for reading how far off the RTC is from NTP time, and another call that can initiate a write to the RTC.
There needs to be a way for a parent to register a `onComplete` callback, which is fired and passes in the results.
agent_reply: Implemented [NtpSync.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/NtpSync.h) with the public API and private state needed for the upcoming `.cpp`:
- FreeRTOS task handle/state needed to run on the current core
`NtpSync.cpp` is still empty, so the next step is filling in the behavior behind this interface.

agent_reply: - `NtpSync::start()` now takes `const WifiManager&` and reads `timezone()` / `ntpServer(index)` from it.
- Made `WifiManager::kNtpServerCount` public so `NtpSync` can share the same count.
- starts a FreeRTOS task pinned to the current core
- reads RTC time, reads NTP time, computes RTC offset in seconds
- supports optional RTC write after sync
